steps.title: Identify the sentence boundary
steps.content:
The sentence has two independent clauses: “They were not the first in England to adopt the literary modes of classical antiquity” (complete idea) and “some of the most prominent figures of the earlier Renaissance period were also influenced by ancient Greek and Roman literature” (complete idea).

These must be joined with punctuation strong enough to separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the conjunctive adverb rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses joined by the conjunctive adverb however follow the pattern IC; however, IC: a semicolon before however and a comma after.

Choice C is the option that places the semicolon before however and the comma after, correctly separating the two complete clauses.

The other options either misplace the punctuation (e.g., put the semicolon after however) or use only commas, creating a comma splice.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
